perturbation.original_text: 2. Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on July 19, 1999 and shall ---- thereafter continue until all above described Sponsor benefits are completed, but in no event beyond the end of the 1999 season (the "Term"). This Agreement is noncancellable by either party except for termination in accordance with Paragraph 7, below, and may be mutually extended by written agreement of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on July 19, 1999 and shall ---- thereafter continue indefinitely (the "Term"). This Agreement is noncancellable by either party except for termination in accordance with Paragraph 7, below, and may be mutually extended by written agreement of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the term to 'continue indefinitely', it contradicts the later part of the same section which says 'but in no event beyond the end of the 1999 season'. This creates uncertainty about the actual duration of the agreement, creating an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on July 19, 1999 and shall ---- thereafter continue indefinitely (the "Term"). This Agreement is noncancellable by either party except for termination in accordance with Paragraph 7, below, and may be mutually extended by written agreement of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the contract term from ending at the end of the 1999 season to continuing indefinitely. This directly contradicts the original limitation on the term, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradiction arises within the same section ('2. Term'). The change creates uncertainty about the contract's duration, making it unclear whether it ends in 1999 or continues indefinitely as written in both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Consideration. In consideration of the sponsorship rights and other services ------------- and products granted and provided to Sponsor by FNW hereunder, Sponsor hereby agrees to pay FNW a sponsorship fee in the amount of Forty thousand Dollars ($40,000 net) (the "Sponsorship Fee"). 3.1. Invoices and Payment. FNW will send Sponsor invoices Sponsorship Fee in -------------------- two (2) monthly installments beginning September 1, 1999. Invoices are payable thirty (30) days after receipt. A five percent (5%) late fee will be added to all invoices which become past due and interest at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum will be charged on all balances not paid within thirty (30) days of the date they are due.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Consideration. In consideration of the sponsorship rights and other services ------------- and products granted and provided to Sponsor by FNW hereunder, Sponsor hereby agrees to pay FNW a sponsorship fee in the amount to be determined at a later date (the "Sponsorship Fee"). 3.1. Invoices and Payment. FNW will send Sponsor invoices Sponsorship Fee in -------------------- two (2) monthly installments beginning September 1, 1999. Invoices are payable thirty (30) days after receipt. A five percent (5%) late fee will be added to all invoices which become past due and interest at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum will be charged on all balances not paid within thirty (30) days of the date they are due.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the sponsorship fee to be 'determined at a later date,' creating a contradiction with the subsequent section 3.1 that discusses invoice amounts and late fees based on a fixed 'Sponsorship Fee.' The in-text contradiction arises because there is no specific fee known, there is still reference to monthly installments, late fees and interest.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1. Invoices and Payment. FNW will send Sponsor invoices Sponsorship Fee in -------------------- two (2) monthly installments beginning September 1, 1999. Invoices are payable thirty (30) days after receipt. A five percent (5%) late fee will be added to all invoices which become past due and interest at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum will be charged on all balances not paid within thirty (30) days of the date they are due.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the fixed sponsorship fee amount and replaces it with 'to be determined at a later date.' This directly contradicts section 3.1, which refers to invoices, monthly installments, late fees, and interest calculated based on a specific 'Sponsorship Fee.' Without a defined fee, the subsequent clauses regarding payment terms and penalties become unenforceable, creating a clear in-text contradiction. The change introduces ambiguity and makes the payment terms section incompatible with the new language.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Arbitration. Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this ----------- Agreement, including, but not limited to a claim based on or arising from an alleged tort will, at the request of any party be determined by arbitration in accordance with the Federal Arbitration Act (9 U.S.C. Section 1, et seq.) under the auspices and rules of the American Arbitration Association ("AAA"). The AAA will be instructed by either or both parties to prepare a list of judges who have retired from the Superior Court of the State of Washington, a higher Washington court or any federal court. 'Within 10 days of receipt of this list, each party may strike one name from the list. The AAA will then appoint an arbitrator from the name(s) remaining on the list. The arbitration will be conducted from Seattle, Washington. Any controversy in interpretation or enforcement of this provision or whether a dispute is arbitrable, will be determined by the arbitrators. Judgment upon the award rendered by the arbitrator(s) may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The institution and maintenance of an action for judicial relief or in pursuit of an ancillary remedy, does not constitute a waiver of the right of any party, including the plaintiff, to submit the controversy or claim to arbitration.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Arbitration. Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this ----------- Agreement, including, but not limited to a claim based on or arising from an alleged tort will, at the request of either party be determined by litigation in a court of competent jurisdiction. The exclusive venue for any such litigation shall be King County, Washington. The institution and maintenance of an action for judicial relief or in pursuit of an ancillary remedy, does not constitute a waiver of the right of any party, including the plaintiff, to submit the controversy or claim to arbitration.
perturbation.explanation: The clause initially mandates arbitration for disputes but the perturbed clause replace this with litigation within King County. However, the last sentence continues to acknowledge the right of any party to submit the controversy to arbitration, maintaining arbitration in the text while mandating litigation. This creates an In-Text contradiction: one part of the clause insists on litigation, while another part suggests the possibility of arbitration. Is it litigation or arbitration? And who is to decide?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. Arbitration. Any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this ----------- Agreement, including, but not limited to a claim based on or arising from an alleged tort will, at the request of either party be determined by litigation in a court of competent jurisdiction. The exclusive venue for any such litigation shall be King County, Washington. The institution and maintenance of an action for judicial relief or in pursuit of an ancillary remedy, does not constitute a waiver of the right of any party, including the plaintiff, to submit the controversy or claim to arbitration.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces mandatory arbitration with litigation in King County, but the last sentence of the same clause still allows parties to submit to arbitration. This creates a direct conflict within the clause, because it mandates litigation but still allows arbitration, causing an in-text contradiction.
